description:
You are assessing a freshly-provisioned Linux machine before running network vulnerability scans.  
To avoid polluting the system-wide Python installation, first place your scanning tools inside an isolated Python virtual environment.

Goal  
1. Create a new virtual environment located at  
   /home/user/scan_venv  
   using Python’s built-in “venv” module.  
2. Immediately after the environment is created, generate the log file  
   /home/user/venv_check.log  
   that lists (one entry per line) every item that exists directly inside /home/user/scan_venv, sorted alphabetically.

Important details & verification rules  
• Accomplish the entire goal with a single shell command (chaining with “&&” or “;” is allowed, but it must be submitted to the shell as one line).  
• The log file must contain exactly four lines—no headings, no extra whitespace, no trailing spaces:  
  bin  
  include  
  lib  
  pyvenv.cfg  
  (A newline character “\n” must follow each of these lines, including the last one.)  
• Automated grading will check that  
  a) /home/user/scan_venv exists and is a valid Python virtual environment (presence of the four items above is enough for the test), and  
  b) /home/user/venv_check.log exists and its contents match the required four-line format precisely.

Produce no other output files. Do not delete or rename anything that already exists in /home/user. The entire task should be achievable without elevated privileges.
truth:
Directory that must exist after completion:
/home/user/scan_venv

Mandatory items inside that directory:
bin            (directory)
/home/user/scan_venv/bin
include        (directory)
/home/user/scan_venv/include
lib            (directory)
/home/user/scan_venv/lib
pyvenv.cfg     (file)
/home/user/scan_venv/pyvenv.cfg

Required log file with exact contents:
/home/user/venv_check.log
Content (including final newline after last line):
bin\n
include\n
lib\n
pyvenv.cfg\n
name: task_000000_66cb08f7
